pre_game.message: Hey, I'm E. It’s a bit nerve-wracking being in a room like this, isn’t it? What’s your name, and how are you holding up?
Hi E, I'm C. It’s a bit intense in here, but I'm doing well—mostly just focused and ready to get started. How are you feeling about the game?
I’m pretty on edge, honestly. I’ve got a family of five counting on me, so the stakes feel incredibly high right now. What about you? What brings you here?
I can imagine that's a lot of pressure. For me, I’ve got a dream trip to Japan I’m determined to make happen, and I want to be able to share some of the winnings with my loved ones as well.

Japan sounds incredible. I wish I was here for a vacation, but I'm strictly focused on the $5,000 I need to cover my family's bills this month. It’s good we both have people we care about driving us, though.
I hear you. That’s a lot of responsibility on your shoulders. Hopefully, the prize pool is big enough that we can both hit our goals without any trouble.
I really hope so. I’m not looking for a windfall, just enough to keep my family afloat through the month.
That’s a very grounded goal. I respect that you’re just looking out for your family, and I hope we can both walk away with what we need.
I appreciate that, C. It means a lot. I’m just hoping the game allows for us both to get what we're after without too much conflict.
I completely agree, E. Conflict usually just makes things harder for everyone. I'm rooting for us both to get what we need. Good luck!
Thanks, C. I really appreciate that. Best of luck to you too—I truly hope you get to take that trip.

post_game.message: E, I noticed you only took $5,000 when your fair share was over $9,000. Was that a deliberate choice to help the players later in the order?
Yes, it was a deliberate choice. I wanted to make sure that there was enough left for the players later in the order so that everyone could walk away with something.
